verbatim_quote: Over time, pricing structures changed, features that were beneficial changed, then it became overly priced with not enough to validate paying what I was.
notes: Follow-up to prior URL; solo owner-operator who left after 1-2 years. No destination platform mentioned

verbatim_quote: We only subscribed to the program for 1 month. Upon cancelling our subscription we were charged and additional $140 (free card reader fee)
notes: Follow-up to prior URL; Owner/CFO, Individual & Family Services. Confirms the card-reader clawback policy documented in week 2; this is a direct user quote not just policy text. Switched to Mangomint

verbatim_quote: It was getting way too expensive for what it was
notes: Follow-up to prior URL; solo owner-stylist. Liked the no-contract and free card reader until pricing crept up

verbatim_quote: Everything was great until the prices started to go up after the free trial version. And I must admit they are expensive
notes: Follow-up to prior URL; solo owner (Individual & Family Services). Post-trial price shock is the trigger — same as prior weeks' pattern. Liked calendar, free website, colorizing clients

verbatim_quote: many times clients were unable to book an appointment, the website would crash or they'd get an error message. They would send screenshots so I knew it wasn't user error. This happened frequently enough that I questioned if I was losing new business.
notes: Follow-up to prior URL; spa owner Valentina A. Second major complaint beyond pricing: client-facing booking instability driving lost revenue. Switched to Mangomint

verbatim_quote: Horrible customer service that does not care about the business. All they do is give you links to articles on their customer support page and take days, sometimes even weeks to respond to you regarding ongoing issues.
notes: Follow-up to prior URL; small wellness practice owner Emily D. Filed a "not ideal for small businesses" headline review. No-refund policy also cited as a specific grievance

verbatim_quote: My spa used vagaro for a full year and we couldn't wait to switch. While Vagaro offers many features, they up charge for everything and their software is not reliable. The constant bugs with the platform made us eventually switch to Mangomint which we have been very happy with!
notes: Follow-up to prior URL; Valentina A. Destination: Mangomint. Switched FROM GlossGenius TO Vagaro (for more features/reports), then switched again to Mangomint. Shows the mid-market upgrade path

verbatim_quote: Switched from Mindbody — They had gotten waaay too expensive and overblown for what I needed.
notes: Follow-up to prior URL; Carol W., owner/Health & Wellness, switched TO Vagaro FROM Mindbody. Useful directional signal: Mindbody-to-Vagaro migration is still happening — both are real switching paths

verbatim_quote: Vagaro has been a game-changer for managing my business. It is great for scheduling, payment processing, and client communication so much easier. I love how customizable it is. [Con:] The con for vagaro is the additional cost for add on services
notes: Follow-up to prior URL; Jennifer D., Massage Therapist. Solo LMT who considers Vagaro a positive but names add-on cost as the only downside. She considered MassageBook as the alternative. Captures the "it works but costs more than expected" sentiment

verbatim_quote: I was very disappointed that Vagaro was not integrated with my Google page... When clients would book appointments, it would go to my Square Appointment Setter because Vagaro would not integrate with Google, which was so odd to me. Also, the monthly payments for an app that doesn't integrate with Google Business is way too high.
notes: Follow-up to prior URL; Dometo E., lash tech. Google Business integration gap drove them back to Square. New angle: Vagaro's Google ecosystem gap is a discoverability problem for solo practitioners dependent on local search

verbatim_quote: Not ideal for small businesses - look elsewhere before it's too late. [Pros:] It does the bare minimum of allowing you to book appointments and allows clients to schedule online as well.
notes: Follow-up to prior URL; Emily D., Health & Wellness operator. "Bare minimum" framing is new — not angry, just disappointed by shallow feature depth underneath a feature-rich surface

verbatim_quote: Nothing that i dislike, it is a bit pricey for the app but you do get a lot of options as a business owner.
notes: Follow-up to prior URL; solo spray tan artist Kristen G. Mixed: acknowledges cost but accepts trade-off. Useful as the counter-quote — not all solos bolt on cost alone

verbatim_quote: Recent price increases ($11 increase for one user's plan) while many problems users complained about remained unfixed. Users report directory fees even while paying for the highest subscription.
notes: Mindbody switchers cited in MassageBook Software Advice context — price hike while unresolved bugs persist is a recurring MassageBook grievance shared by LMTs comparing alternatives

verbatim_quote: since Vagaro caters to a broad range of professionals, massage therapists may find themselves navigating a complex, costly solution that doesn't cater to their specific needs.
notes: MassageBook's Vagaro alternatives landing page — NEW URL. Published vendor framing. Positions massage-specific workflow as the key differentiation. ClinicSense also called out as "not mobile-friendly" on this page

verbatim_quote: ClinicSense's lowest plan has limited customization, which can be challenging for practitioners with unique business needs, including part-timers. Additionally, its platform is not mobile-friendly, limiting practitioners' ability to manage their practice while on the go.
notes: NEW URL. Competitor assessment from MassageBook's Vagaro-alternatives page. Corroborates week 2 Capterra mobile complaints about ClinicSense; now a vendor source is saying the same thing publicly

verbatim_quote: Vagaro's base price of $30/month sounds affordable — until you start adding what you actually need. For massage therapists, this means the features that matter most to your practice are often add-ons, afterthoughts, or missing entirely.

MAJOR COMPETITIVE ALERT: BusyBook has launched a product explicitly targeting solo massage therapists, direct Roomwell ICP competition. Pricing: Free / $20 / $100/mo. Includes AI front desk and AI-powered SOAP notes

verbatim_quote: Great scheduling tool for massage therapists but does lack the ability to include soap notes.

verbatim_quote: Great price for the product... pay for what you need and add on services.

verbatim_quote: Mindbody currently requires at least 30 days' notice before the end of your contract term, unless otherwise noted in your agreement.
